blocks.md: A comparison gives `1` or `0`. To choose between two values, index a two-item list with `1 + condition`. If the condition is `0`, the index is 1. If the condition is `1`, the index is 2.
Another idiom uses arithmetic on a mask. `⍵ × ⍵ > 0` keeps positive values and turns the others into zero.
`∊` (membership) asks whether each left item appears in the right argument. `'hangman' ∊ 'an'` marks the letters of the word that have been guessed.

blocks.prompt: Define `sign` so a positive argument gives `'pos'` and anything else gives `'neg'`. Use the pick idiom. It will be tested on 5 and should give `pos`.
blocks.hint: 1 + ⍵ > 0 produces index 1 or 2 for the pair 'neg' 'pos'

blocks.prompt: Use a mask, not `⌈`, to turn `¯3 4 ¯1 6 ¯2` into `0 4 0 6 0`. Keep positives and zero the rest.
blocks.hint: multiply each value by its > 0 Boolean

blocks.prompt: Mark which letters of `'banana'` appear in the guessed set `'an'`. Use `1` for guessed and `0` for not.
blocks.hint: use the word as the left argument and the guessed letters as the right argument

blocks.prompt: For `1 2 3 4 5 6`, mark which items are members of `2 3 5`.
blocks.hint: left ∊ right tests each left item against the whole right argument
